title: Sustainable, Resilient, and Just Global Clean Energy Supply Chains
text:
Overview
This research theme aims at evidence-based analysis to understand the importance of a sustainable and resilient clean energy global supply chains to achieve climate goals. We evaluate the economic costs, carbon mitigation, air quality, and human health benefits achieved by global clean energy supply chains.

title: Deep Policy Lab
text:
We use data-drive, evidence-base approach to study energy and climate policy and offer policy insights for energy decisions and climate actions to achieve carbon neutrality.



Learn more

title: About Deep Policy Lab
text:
The Deep Energy and Climate Policy Lab (Deep Policy Lab) provides data-driven, evidence based, energy and climate policy analysis and research to support energy decisions and climate actions.
Deep Policy Lab aims to advance actionable and evidence-based energy and climate solutions through data-driven analysis and dialogue. Deep Policy Lab leverages big data, machine learning, and systems modeling to inform policy decisions on renewable energy, clean energy supply chains, deep decarbonization, and other topics related to the energy transition and carbon neutrality.
Deep Policy Lab collaborates with academic institutions, government agencies, industry partners, and civil society organizations to foster innovation and impact in the field of energy and climate policy.

title: Power Systems Carbon Neutrality
text:
Overview
Electrifying energy, and decarbonizing electricity. Power system is at the center of global energy transtion. This research theme explores the pathways and their environmental, climate, human health, and socio-economic implications.

title: Power Systems Carbon Neutrality
text:
Featured publications
He, Gang*, Jiang Lin*, Froylan Sifuentes, Xu Liu, Nikit Abhyankar, and Amol Phadke*. 2020. Rapid Cost Decrease of Renewables and Storage Accelerates the Decarbonization of China’s Power System. Nature Communications 11 (1): 2486. doi: 10.1038/s41467-020-16184-x. [pdf]
Lin, Jiang*, Nikit Abhyankar, Gang He, Xu Liu, and Shengfei Yin. 2022. Large Balancing Areas and Dispersed Renewable Investment Enhances Grid Flexibility in a Renewable-Dominant Power System in China. iScience 25(2): 103749. doi: 10.1016/j.isci.2022.103749. [pdf]
Li, Bo, Minyou Chen*, Ziming Ma, Gang He, Wei Dai, Dongran Liu, Chi Zhang, and Haiwang Zhong. 2022. Modelling Integrated Power and Transportation Sectors Decarbonization with Hydrogen Energy Storage. IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications 58(2): 2677–2693. doi: 10.1109/TIA.2021.3116916. [pdf]
He, Gang*, Anne-Perrine Avrin, James H. Nelson, Josiah Johnston, Ana Mileva, Jianwei Tian, and Daniel M. Kammen*. 2016. SWITCH-China:A Systems Approach to Decarbonizing China’s Power System. Environmental Science and Technology 50(11): 5467–5473. doi: 10.1021/acs.est.6b01345. [pdf]
